Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-12-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:

John N. Heffner, Esq., for Motorola, Inc., Defendant/Moving Party
Lisa J. Espada, Esq., for Donna Fay Bomnar, Plaintiff/Opposing Party.

Tentative Ruling adopted: Defendant Motorola, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Discovery Responses is granted as the objections are ordered stricken. The responses are inadequate as they reference other interrogatory responses. Each response must be separate and individual. While there is some merit to the argument that the purpose of the General Order Interrogatories is undermined by many of these interrogatories, there is no authority for the proposition that make those responses excuses a party from responding to further discovery. Responses which identify witnesses generically as having been identified in depositions are also inadequate. Responses which reserve the right to supplement and/or amend are improper. C.C.P. Section 2030.310 sets forth the procedures relating to amendments and subsequently discovered information. The court advises counsel that in many other respects, the responses are otherwise adequate.
Further responsesl shall be made within 20 days of notice of this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
